items.title: Burdett 'Burd' Sisler, war veteran and oldest known living Canadian, has died at 110
items.summary_auto: Burdett "Burd" Sisler, the oldest known living Canadian — and one of the world's oldest Second World War veterans — has died at 110, his family confirmed on Saturday.

items.title: Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen talks about 'incredible' views of Earth in live video call from deep space
items.summary_auto: The virtual event featured Hansen speaking live from aboard NASA's Orion spacecraft, approximately three days into the 10-day lunar flyby mission.

items.title: When did Easter become 'spring Christmas'? Stressed parents would like to know
items.summary_auto: If it seems like every year, Easter, and in fact every holiday, gets a little more intense, we're here to tell you that you're correct.

items.title: How the Middle East war is already impacting mortgage rates in Canada
items.summary_auto: The ongoing war in Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz are impacting Canadian pocketbooks in ways we might not expect. Experts warn Canadian homeowners facing mortgage renewals that rates are going up, despite the Bank of Canada holding its key interest rate for months.

items.title: Darjeeling is world renowned. But demand for 'champagne of teas' is going flat as drought dulls its flavour
items.summary_auto: An extremely dry winter has made for another challenging harvest of India's famous Darjeeling tea, with the flavour the region is renowned for at risk as quality dips. As climate change makes the weather more erratic in the Himalayan foothills, estate owners are growing ever more worried about the future of their businesses.

items.title: It's a World Series rematch as the Dodgers return to Toronto. Here's what the Jays are up against
items.summary_auto: The Los Angeles Dodgers are back in Toronto to take on the Blue Jays for a three-game series starting Monday night. What's changed since they defeated the Jays to become World Series champs in Toronto last year? Let's find out.

items.title: Smoky, fast-moving wildfire triggers evacuation orders in Southern California
items.summary_auto: The fire was burning in a populated — but not densely so — unincorporated part of Riverside County, in a recreational area near the city of Moreno Valley, which has a population of roughly 200,000.

items.title: #TheMoment an escaped kangaroo spent 3 days on the run
items.summary_auto: Debbie Marland, owner of Sunshine Farms, tells The National about the moment Chesney the kangaroo escaped and spent three days on the run after being spooked by hunting dogs.

items.title: RCMP say damage to police vehicles in First Nations protest includes 'urine-soaked interior surfaces'
Trump administration seeks $152M to reopen Alcatraz as 'state-of-the-art secure prison'
items.summary_auto: The White House released a budget on Friday, in which the Trump administration requested funds for the ⁠Federal Bureau ⁠of Prisons to cover the ‌first-year costs of rebuilding Alcatraz into "a state-of-the-art secure prison facility."

items.title: Is male friendship in trouble? Actor Andrew McCarthy went on a road trip to find out
items.summary_auto: After the author and "Brat Pack" actor's wife and son confronted him with the fact he didn't have friends anymore, McCarthy crisscrossed the country to visit old friends and ask strangers whether friendships between men are in trouble.

items.title: Israel strikes Beirut as U.S. warns Iran may target universities
items.summary_auto: Israel pounded Beirut with airstrikes on Friday that the military said ‌had targeted what it calls militant "infrastructure" in Lebanon's capital, as the U.S. warned that Iran may target universities in the country as war in the Middle East rages.

items.title: 2 deaths after giving plasma in Winnipeg not linked to donation process: Health Canada
items.summary_auto: The federal health regulator said Friday its assessment found no evidence of a machine malfunction and the equipment performed as expected, wrote Health Canada spokesperson Mark Johnson in an emailed statement.

items.title: Toronto Tempo select guard Julie Allemand as 1st-ever player in WNBA expansion draft
items.summary_auto: The Toronto Tempo selected Julie Allemand with their first pick of the WNBA expansion draft on Friday.
